factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to supply chain disruptions, higher commodity and component costs, and increased freight logistics expenses. While the company has implemented pricing and productivity initiatives to partially offset these increases, the overall impact remains challenging with potential material adverse effects on business operations.

evidence.text: During 2021 and 2022, we have experienced multiple disruptions to our supply chain. This disruption has resulted, and may continue to result, in sufficient inventory not being available in a timely manner or during the appropriate season as well as higher freight and other logistic costs, including increased carrier rates, which could have a material adverse effect on our business.
evidence.subsection: Cybersecurity incidents could disrupt business operations, result in the loss of critical and confidential information, and adversely impact our reputation and results of operations.

evidence.text: These amounts were partially offset by the higher costs of commodities and components used in our products and higher freight and logistic costs.

Each of our segments continue to be impacted by the higher cost of commodities and components used in our products, certain supply chain constraints and higher freight costs. sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs despite partial offsetting measures.

factors.summary: Demand trends show mixed performance across different segments. While the Refrigeration segment experienced flat organic net sales due to challenges in end markets, the HVAC segment demonstrated strong growth with a 12% organic increase driven by pricing improvements and robust end-market demand.

evidence.text: Organic Net sales were flat compared to the prior year as each of the segment's businesses experienced challenges in certain end markets during the second half of the year. Results for Commercial refrigeration were flat compared with the prior year...

The organic increase in Net sales of 12% was driven by continued strong results across each of the segment's businesses. Increased sales in our North America residential and light commercial business (up 14%) were primarily driven by pricing improvements and end market demand.

factors.summary: The company's margins are facing mixed pressures. While the Refrigeration Segment has seen margin expansion due to pricing improvements and productivity initiatives, overall gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher input and logistics costs, despite efforts to offset these through pricing.

evidence.text: Significant negative industry or economic trends, disruptions to our business... may result in the impairment of goodwill or intangible assets...
evidence.subsection: We may recognize impairment charges for our goodwill and intangible assets.

evidence.text: The increase in operational profit of 5% was primarily attributable to pricing improvements compared with the prior year. In addition, favorable productivity initiatives and lower selling, general and administrative costs further benefited operational profit. These amounts were partially offset by the higher costs of commodities and components used in our products and higher freight and logistic costs.

Gross margin as a percentage of Net sales decreased by 220 basis points compared with the same period of 2021. Although pricing improvements more than offset inflationary impacts and supply chain challenges, gross margin as a percentage of Net sales decreased by 220 basis points compared with the same period of 2021.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure due to higher input and logistics costs despite pricing improvements.

factors.summary: The revenue mix has shifted towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Fire & Security and HVAC sectors, driven by pricing improvements and strong demand. This shift has positively impacted both the organic growth and operating margins of these segments.

evidence.text: The organic increase in Net sales of 5% was primarily driven by pricing improvements compared with the prior year. Global industrial sales also benefited segment results with pricing improvements and strong demand.
The Fire & Security segment's operating margin increased significantly from 12.0% to 45.7%. This significant growth highlights the effectiveness of the strategies implemented in this segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix has shifted towards higher-margin segments, driving organic growth and operating margins.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power across its segments, with pricing improvements contributing to higher operational profits and successfully offsetting inflationary impacts. Evidence highlights the effectiveness of pricing strategies in driving profitability and mitigating cost increases.

evidence.text: The increase in operational profit of 5% was primarily attributable to pricing improvements compared with the prior year. In addition, favorable productivity initiatives and lower selling, general and administrative costs further benefited operational profit.

Pricing improvements more than offset inflationary impacts in each of our segments. The organic increase was primarily driven by our HVAC segment due to pricing improvements in our North America residential and light commercial business and improved global end-markets in our Commercial HVAC business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power is evident as pricing improvements offset inflationary impacts and drive profitability.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different segments and regions. While the HVAC segment experiences growth in North America and Asia, driven by increased sales and end-market demand, the Refrigeration Segment faces challenges with stable to slightly declining volumes in Europe due to economic conditions and supply chain constraints.

evidence.text: Results for Commercial refrigeration were flat compared with the prior year, primarily driven by continued supply chain constraints and lower volumes in Europe... In addition, ongoing growth in Asia was more than offset by fourth quarter COVID-19 impacts.

Increased sales in our North America residential and light commercial business (up 14%) were primarily driven by pricing improvements and end market demand. These amounts were partially offset by volume reductions in certain end markets.

factors.summary: The company's organic growth shows mixed performance across different segments. While the Refrigeration segment maintained stable organic growth, the Fire & Security segment experienced a deceleration after excluding the impact of the Chubb divestiture. Overall, organic revenue growth accelerated to an 8% increase in 2022 compared to 2021, driven by pricing improvements and demand for energy-efficient products.

evidence.text: Organic Net sales were flat compared to the prior year... Absent the results of Chubb, Net sales increased 6% from $3.3 billion to $3.6 billion.

Organic sales for the year ended December 31, 2022 increased by 8% compared with the same period of 2021. We continue to benefit from the demand for energy-efficient, digital products and healthy building solutions. In addition, pricing improvements more than offset inflationary impacts in each of our segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company achieved an 8% organic revenue growth in 2022, driven by pricing improvements and demand for energy-efficient products.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction and efficiency programs that have positively impacted operational profits in certain areas, such as the Refrigeration Segment. However, in the context of supply chain challenges, while investments are being made to enhance resilience through automation and dual sourcing, there is limited evidence of measurable savings or a clear trajectory of their impact.

evidence.text: Favorable productivity initiatives and lower selling, general and administrative costs further benefited operational profit.

We continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible. To date, there has been limited disruption to the availability of our products, though it is possible that more significant disruptions could occur if these supply chain challenges continue.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While cost reduction programs positively impacted certain areas, the evidence is mixed regarding their overall measurable impact due to ongoing supply chain challenges.

factors.summary: End-market momentum is mixed across the Fire & Security and HVAC segments. While there is growth in North America, Asia, and Europe for the Fire & Security segment, particularly in the Americas and Europe, the segment faces challenges in China. The HVAC segment shows strong growth in North America and Asia, but faces tempered growth in Europe due to economic conditions and inflationary pressures. The Refrigeration segment experiences weakness in certain end markets, driven by supply chain constraints and lower volumes.

evidence.text: The segment primarily saw growth in both residential and commercial sales in the Americas and Europe as sales in China decreased as a result of current economic conditions and reduced end-market demand. Results for Commercial refrigeration were flat compared with the prior year, primarily driven by continued supply chain constraints and lower volumes in Europe...
The business saw growth in North America and Asia while growth in Europe was tempered by current economic conditions and inflationary cost pressures which impacted end-market demand.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End-market momentum is mixed across segments, with some growth and some challenges, indicating a balanced view.

evidence.text: We continue to actively manage and strengthen our business and product portfolio to meet the current and future needs of our customers. This is accomplished through research and development activities with a focus on new product development and new technology innovation as well as sustaining activities with a focus on improving existing products and reducing production costs.
evidence.subsection: Summary of Other Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: Research and development costs relate to new product development and new technology innovation. [...] We continue to invest to prepare for future energy efficiency and refrigerant regulation changes and in digital controls technologies.

factors.summary: The company is actively investing in digital technologies and automation to enhance efficiency, innovation, and supply chain resilience. These efforts are directed at both product development and operational improvements, including reducing production costs and enhancing supply chain resilience through automation and dual sourcing.

evidence.text: This is accomplished through research and development activities with a focus on new product development and new technology innovation as well as sustaining activities with a focus on improving existing products and reducing production costs.
evidence.subsection: Summary of Other Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: We continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: The company is actively developing new products, particularly focusing on sustainable and intelligent solutions, which are gaining market traction and contributing to revenue growth through recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: We have set goals to invest over $2 billion by 2030 to develop healthy, safe, sustainable and intelligent buildings and cold chain solutions that incorporate sustainable design principles and reduce lifecycle impacts.
evidence.subsection: Summary of Other Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: The acquisition included all of TCC's advanced research and development centers and global manufacturing operations, product pipeline and the long-term use of Toshiba's iconic brand.
